documents.title: Florida International University public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: FIU is positioning AI as part of Miami's technology ecosystem through AI 305, computing research, FINDS digital forensics, AI-enabled health research, and business/industry engagement.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: Florida International University's public AI policy posture is permissive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: FINDS Digital Forensics Center of Excellence
bodies.scope: FIU is positioning AI as part of Miami's technology ecosystem through AI 305, computing research, FINDS digital forensics, AI-enabled health research, and business/industry engagement.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: Florida International University has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires privacy, academic-integrity, and human-oversight controls as AI use scales.

initiatives.description: FIU publishes generative-AI resources for faculty and specific AI assessment-design guidance, including assessment practices intended to adapt course work to generative AI.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for FL. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: Florida International University is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: FIU is positioning AI as part of Miami's technology ecosystem through AI 305, computing research, FINDS digital forensics, AI-enabled health research, and business/industry engagement. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.
No total institution-wide AI investment amount was found.
Exact enterprise AI user counts or contract values were usually not public.
FIU has public AI assessment guidance and Gemini support documentation, but contract values, active-user counts, and a Research HPC GPU count were not public in searched sources.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: governance led.
